KB4488535 - Cumulative Update 14 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard
Release Date: March 19, 2019
Version: 13.0.4560.0
This article describes cumulative update package 14 (build number: 13.0.4560.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
12491205 FIX: Intermittent failures when you back up to Azure storage from SQL Server 2014, 2016 SQL Engine
and 2017 (KB4463320)
12656913 FIX: Assertion occurs when a parallel query deletes from a Filestream table in SQL Server SQL Engine
2014 and 2016 (KB4488809)
12677809 FIX: Filtered NCI over a CCI may not be maintained when the table is updated in a way that SQL
none of the key or included columns of the NCI are changed (KB4490138) performance
12750916 FIX: "The given key was not present in the dictionary" occurs if you do a preview in the Integration
CDC Source in SQL Server 2016 (KB4490435) Services
12673774 FIX: Floating point exception error 3628 occurs when you query DMV In-Memory
sys.dm_db_xtp_hash_index_stats in SQL Server 2016 (KB4491696) OLTP
12644869 FIX: Error occurs when sp_addarticle is used to add article for transactional replication to SQL Engine
memory-optimized table on subscriber in SQL Server 2016 (KB4493329)
12746393 FIX: A specially crafted query run by a low privileged user may expose the masked data in SQL security
SQL Server 2016 (KB4493363)
How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the

--- Page 1143 of 2000 ---
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology

--- Page 1144 of 2000 ---
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components
(features). However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently
installed on the SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is
added to the instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

KB4475775 - Cumulative Update 13 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence
Release Date: January 23, 2019
Version: 13.0.4550.1
This article describes cumulative update package 13 (build number: 13.0.4550.1) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
12488504 FIX: Missing logs for Analysis Services Processing tasks in SQL Server 2016 and 2017 Analysis
Integration Services (KB4055674) services
12488510 FIX: Memory gets exhausted when you run Power BI report that executes DAX query on Analysis
SSAS 2014, 2016 and 2017 Multidimensional mode (KB4090032) services
12488518 FIX: Error occurs when you run sp_send_dbmail stored procedure that contains comma in Management
sender email address and name in SQL Server (KB4346803) Tools
12482227 FIX: Access violation when you run a query that uses the XML data type in SQL Server XML
2014, 2016 and 2017 (KB4460112)
12557137 FIX: Assertion error occurs when you run a MERGE statement with an OUTPUT clause in SQL Engine
SQL Server 2016 and 2017 (KB4465745)
12578032 FIX: "Non-yielding" error occurs when there is a heavy use of prepared statements in SQL SQL Engine
Server 2014 and 2016 (KB4475322)
12575120 FIX: Error 18204 during automatic backup in virtual machines when the backup file is split Management
into multiple files (KB4480709) Tools
12507748 FIX: Access violation when you run a query that contains a batch-mode hash join on SQL Engine
Clustered Columnstore Index table in SQL Server 2016 (KB4486852)
12210652 FIX: Users are incorrectly permitted to create incremental statistics on nonclustered SQL
indexes that are not aligned to the base table in SQL Server 2016 (KB4486932) performance
How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.

--- Page 1189 of 2000 ---
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note

--- Page 1190 of 2000 ---
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components
(features). However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently
installed on the SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is
added to the instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file

information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

--- Page 1234 of 2000 ---
KB4464343 - Cumulative Update 12 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence
Release Date: November 13, 2018
Version: 13.0.4541.0
This article describes cumulative update package 12 (build number: 13.0.4541.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
12176017 FIX: Deadlock when run the "CleanOrphanedPolicies" and "DeleteDataSources" built-in Reporting Services
stored procedures together in SSRS 2016 (KB4019799)
12180164 FIX: Access violation when you try to access a table when page compression is enabled SQL Engine
on the table in SQL Server (KB4294694)
12361234 FIX: Incorrect results occur when you convert "pollinginterval" parameter from seconds SQL Engine
to hours in sys.sp_cdc_scan in SQL Server (KB4459220)
12458030 FIX: Access violation occurs and SSAS crashes when you process an SSAS database in Analysis Services
SQL Server 2014 and 2016 (KB4459981)
12458027 FIX: Overestimations when using default Cardinality Estimator to query table with many SQL performance
null values (KB4460116)
12199266 FIX: The "modification_counter" in DMV sys.dm_db_stats_properties shows incorrect SQL Engine
value when partitions are merged through ALTER PARTITION in SQL Server 2016
(KB4465443)
11179896 FIX: Assertion error occurs when you run a MERGE statement with an OUTPUT clause in SQL Engine
SQL Server 2016 and 2017 (KB4465745)
12399978 FIX: Snapshot Agent fails when you publish many tables using snapshot or transactional Management
replication (KB4465747) Tools
12458041 FIX: "ran out of memory" error when executing a query on a table that has a large full- SQL Engine
text index in SQL Server 2014 and 2016 (KB4465867)

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.

--- Page 1236 of 2000 ---
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components
(features). However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently
installed on the SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is
added to the instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update

--- Page 1237 of 2000 ---
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4459676 - Cumulative Update 11 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence
Release Date: September 17, 2018
Version: 13.0.4528.0
This article describes cumulative update package 11 (build number: 13.0.4528.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
12180193 FIX: Circular dependency between column names for a calculated table that summarizes the Analysis
partition query dataset in SQL Server (KB4338204) Services
12177840 FIX: A memory leak occurs in sqlwepxxx.dll causes the WmiPrvSe.exe process to crash SQL Engine
(KB4133191)
12278211 FIX: Access violations and unhandled exceptions when you set automatic seeding for High
secondary replica or Distributed Availability Group replica in SQL Server (KB4457953) Availability
12319581 FIX: "9003 error, sev 20, state 1" error when a backup operation fails on a secondary replica SQL Engine
that is running in asynchronous-commit mode (KB4458880)
12177846 FIX: Thai characters in SSAS for SQL Server exported to a PDF file are displayed incorrectly Reporting
(KB4131251) Services
12267691 FIX: Masked data is exposed when a query that uses sp_cursorfetch is run in SQL Server if SQL security
Dynamic Data Masking is enabled (KB4459535)
12264620 FIX: Error occurs when encryption is enabled on a database that uses an Azure Key vault in SQL security
SQL Server 2016 (KB4463125)
12043568 FIX: "An unexpected exception occurred" when you run an MDX query after you run an Analysis
XMLA query to process a dimension in SSAS (KB4463328) Services
12228212 Improves error messages 1412 and 1478 by adding LSN values (BackupLSN, FlushedLSN, High
RedoLSN, TruncationLSN, HardenedLSN). Availability
How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.

--- Page 1281 of 2000 ---
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note

--- Page 1282 of 2000 ---
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components
(features). However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently
installed on the SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is
added to the instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file

--- Page 1283 of 2000 ---
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

--- Page 1326 of 2000 ---
KB4341569 - Cumulative Update 10 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence
Release Date: July 16, 2018
Version: 13.0.4514.0
This article describes cumulative update package 10 (build number: 13.0.4514.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:

--- Page 1328 of 2000 ---
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components
(features). However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently
installed on the SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is
added to the instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.

--- Page 1329 of 2000 ---
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

KB4100997 - Cumulative Update 9 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: May 30, 2018
Version: 13.0.4502.0
This article describes cumulative update package 9 (build number: 13.0.4502.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the

--- Page 1374 of 2000 ---
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology

--- Page 1375 of 2000 ---
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components (features).
However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently installed on the
SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is added to the
instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

KB4077064 - Cumulative Update 8 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: March 19, 2018
Version: 13.0.4474.0
This article describes cumulative update package 8 (build number: 13.0.4474.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .

--- Page 1423 of 2000 ---
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
Components (features) updated
One Cumulative Update package includes all available updates for ALL SQL Server 2016 components
(features). However, the cumulative update package updates only those components that are currently
installed on the SQL Server instance you select to be serviced. If a SQL Server feature (e.g. Analysis Services) is
added to the instance after this CU is applied, you must re-apply this CU to update the new feature to this CU.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select View installed updates under Programs and Features.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.

--- Page 1424 of 2000 ---
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

--- Page 1467 of 2000 ---
KB4057119 - Cumulative Update 7 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise
to: Core, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web, SQL Server 2016 Business
Intelligence
Release Date: January 04, 2018
Version: 13.0.4466.4
Cumulative Update 7 (CU7) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1) was also released as a SQL
Server Security Bulletin on January 4, 2018, KB4058561. See KB4058561 for more information. In other
words, the same physical package (SQLServer2016-KB4057119-x64.exe) was made available both as CU7
(KB4057119) and the Security Bulletin (KB4058561).
Because of this, you may already have CU7 installed as part of that security bulletin release and installation of
this CU is unnecessary. If you do try to install CU7 after ADV180002, you may receive the following message:
There are no SQL Server instances or shared features that can be updated on this computer
This indicates that CU7 is already installed and no further action is required.
This article describes cumulative update package 7 (build number: 13.0.4466.4) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
11173911 FIX: Assertion occurs when you pass memory-optimized table variable into a stored SQL Engine
procedure as table-valued parameter in SQL Server 2016 (KB4056117)
11199159 FIX: Error 156 when SQL Server replication article contains either SQL Engine
GEOGRAPHY_AUTO_GRID or GEOMETRY_AUTO_GRID (KB4037412)

SQL Server 2016 (KB4056831) Services

Server 2016 (KB4056955)

SQL Server 2016 (KB4057055)

11303628 FIX: Error 14684 when you reconfigure Management Data Warehouse in a named instance Management
of SQL Server 2016 (KB4057190) Tools

multidimensional mode (KB4057759) Services
How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note

--- Page 1469 of 2000 ---
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply an update:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the update on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note

--- Page 1470 of 2000 ---
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply an update in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form displays
the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language, that's because a
cumulative update package isn't available for specifically for that language and the ENU download
applies to all languages.
Components (features) updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package under Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information

--- Page 1471 of 2000 ---
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package may not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a product to
the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues that are listed in
this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4037354 - Cumulative Update 6 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server 2016
to: Standard, SQL Server 2016 Service Pack 1
Release Date: November 21, 2017
Version: 13.0.4457.0
This article describes cumulative update package 6 (build number: 13.0.4457.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
７ Note

Knowledge Base article might not be created for each bug.

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update

--- Page 1517 of 2000 ---
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability,
and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with
Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.

--- Page 1518 of 2000 ---
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form displays
the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language, that's because a
cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that language and the ENU download applies to
all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a product to
the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues that are listed in
this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4040714 - Cumulative Update 5 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server 2016 Standard,
to: SQL Server 2016 Web, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence, SQL Server 2016 Service Pack
1
Release Date: September 18, 2017
Version: 13.0.4451.0
This article describes cumulative update package 5 (build number: 13.0.4451.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016
Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016 SP1.

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.

--- Page 1543 of 2000 ---
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability,
and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with
Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support

--- Page 1544 of 2000 ---
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form displays
the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language, that's because a
cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that language and the ENU download applies to
all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a product to
the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues that are listed in
this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file

--- Page 1545 of 2000 ---
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4024305 - Cumulative Update 4 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Service Pack 1, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server
to: 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: August 8, 2017
Version: 13.0.4446.0
Cumulative Update 4 (CU4) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1) was also released as a
SQL Server Security Bulletin on 8/8/2017, KB4019095 . See CVE-2017-8516 for more information.
Because of this, you may already have CU4 installed as part of that security bulletin release and
installation of this CU is unnecessary. If you do try to install CU4 after CVE-2017-8516, you may receive
the following message:
There are no SQL Server instances or shared features that can be updated on this computer
This indicates that CU4 is already installed and no further action is required.
７ Note
The package name for CU4, "SQLServer2016SP1-KB4024305-<x86/x64>.exe", contains the CVE-
2017-8516 KB number (4019095), not the CU4 KB number, (4024305). This can be ignored as a
single package services both release channels.
This article describes cumulative update package 4 (build number: 13.0.4446.0) for Microsoft SQL Server
2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server
2016 SP1.
Improvements and fixes included in this update

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends
that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available at the Download Center.

--- Page 1570 of 2000 ---
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the
same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already
been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and
mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS
with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form
displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language,
that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that language and the
ENU download applies to all languages.

--- Page 1571 of 2000 ---
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service
request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't
qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service
and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support
website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control
Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues
that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the
following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When
you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local
time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4019916 - Cumulative Update 3 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server 2016
to: Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: May 15, 2017
Version: 13.0.4435.0
This article describes cumulative update package 3 (build number: 13.0.4435.0) for Microsoft SQL Server
2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server
2016 SP1.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
9367899 FIX: KPIs return a non-translated caption when an MDSCHEMA_MEASURES request Analysis Services
is issued and the restrictions request hidden members (KB3198827)
9730123 FIX: The SQL Server Analysis Service encounters an internal error when it processes Analysis Services
a Tabular model (KB4016805)
9782406 FIX: Error when you process a calculated column on a database with compatibility Analysis Services
level 1200 in SSAS 2016 tabular mode (KB4018955)
9782644 FIX: SSAS fails to start if the MemoryHeapType and HeapTypeForObjects properties Analysis Services
are set to -1 (KB4019511)
9813872 FIX: "Partition cannot contain column segments of different data versions" error Analysis Services
when you process data on a partition in SSAS Tabular model (KB4013208)
9646715 FIX: MDX query returns an error in SQL Server Analysis Service if the dimension Analysis Services
table contains members that don't exist in the fact table (KB4018741)
9690463 FIX: SSAS 2016 crashes with an access violation when many partitions and their Analysis Services
aggregations/indexes are processed together concurrently (KB4019988)
9875165 FIX: SSAS crashes when you retrieve KPIs as hidden members by using schema Analysis Services
rowsets in SQL Server (KB4019048)
9813829 FIX: DMV sys.dm_hadr_availability_group_states displays "NOT_HEALTHY" in High Availability
synchronization_health_desc column on secondary replicas in SQL Server
(KB4013111)
9813880 Update enables DML query plan to scan query memory-optimized tables in parallel In-Memory OLTP
in SQL Server 2016 (KB4013877)
9813879 FIX: Out-of-memory error when you run a query to access LOB columns through In- In-Memory OLTP
Memory OLTP in SQL Server 2016 (KB3198751)

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends
that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available at the Download Center.

--- Page 1587 of 2000 ---
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the
same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already
been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and
mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS
with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form
displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language,
that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that language and the
ENU download applies to all languages.

--- Page 1588 of 2000 ---
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service
request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't
qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service
and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support
website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control
Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues
that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the
following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When
you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local
time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4013106 - Cumulative Update 2 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 • Applies to: SQL Server 2016 Service Pack 1
Release Date: March 20, 2017
Version: 13.0.4422.0
This article describes cumulative update package 2 (build number: 13.0.4422.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of
SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Known issues in this update
After installing this CU, Change Data Capture (CDC) functionality might break if:
The databases enabled for CDC are part of Always On availability group (or)
SQL Server replication components are not installed on the server
For more information, see:
CDC functionality may break after upgrading to the latest CU for SQL Server 2012, 2014 and
2016
Special steps for change data capture or replication
Improvements and fixes included in this update
