How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain
the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends
that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.

--- Page 1606 of 2000 ---
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them
to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and
mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about
SSIS with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring
topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form
displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.

--- Page 1607 of 2000 ---
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that
don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer
Service and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft
support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control
Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the
following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).
When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC
and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

KB3208177 - Cumulative Update 1 for SQL
Server 2016 SP1
Article • 07/26/2024 • Applies to: Microsoft SQL Server
Release Date: January 18, 2017
Version: 13.0.4411.0
This article describes cumulative update package 1 (build number: 13.0.4411.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1). This update contains fixes that were released after the release of
SQL Server 2016 SP1.

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain
the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available at the Download
Center.

--- Page 1620 of 2000 ---
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them
to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
７ Note
After you apply this update, Database Mail does not work on computers that do not have the
.NET Framework 3.5 installed. Microsoft is currently working on addressing this in a future
cumulative update. See the following KB article for more information about affected builds and
suggested workarounds:
FIX: SQL Server 2016 Database Mail does not work on a computer that does not have the
.NET Framework 3.5 installed
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and
mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about
SSIS with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.

--- Page 1621 of 2000 ---
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring
topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control
Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016 SP1.
Restart information

--- Page 1622 of 2000 ---
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

--- Page 1630 of 2000 ---
KB3182545 - SQL Server 2016 Service
Pack 1 release information
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core,
to: SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: November 16, 2016
Version: 13.0.4001.0
This article contains important information to read before you install Microsoft SQL
Server 2016 Service Pack 1 (SP1). It describes how to get the service pack, the list of fixes
that are included in the service pack, known issues, and a list of copyright attributions
for the product.
７ Note
This article serves as a single source of information to locate all documentation
that's related to this service pack. It includes all the information that you previously
found in the release notes and Readme.txt files.
Known issues in this service pack
SQL Server Reporting Services
After you install SQL Server 2016 SP1, you may encounter the following issues when you
use SQL Server Reporting Services:
If Reporting Services is set to use a secure (https/SSL) connection, a warning about
security content may be displayed.
In certain scenarios, the Print button stops working.
Install the hotfix in KB 3207512 to address these issues.
SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS)
After you install SQL Server 2016 SP1, the DCOM permissions for launching and
accessing Integration Services service are reset to default permissions. If you have
customized DCOM permissions, you'll need to reapply the customization.

--- Page 1631 of 2000 ---
ODBC Driver 13.1
With the default installation of SQL Server 2016, ODBC Driver 13.0 is installed on the
server which is used by SQL Agent and SSMS (installed on server) to connect to the SQL
Server instance. If you have installed ODBC Driver 13.1 on your server for any reason,
SQL Server 2016 SP1 installation overrides the ODBC Driver 13.1 installation and the
fixes introduced in 13.1 like KB 3185365 may be lost. In this case post installation of SQL
Server 2016 SP1, it's recommended to uninstall the ODBC Driver installed by SQL Server
2016 SP1 and install ODBC Driver 13.1 .
To check for ODBC Driver installed on the server, you can go to Control Panel of the
server -> Programs and Features -> Search for ODBC. The version number of ODBC
Driver 13.1 is 13.1.811.168 as shown below:
List of fixes included in SQL Server 2016 SP1
Microsoft SQL Server 2016 service packs are cumulative updates. SQL Server 2016 SP1
upgrades all editions and service levels of SQL Server 2016 to SQL Server 2016 SP1. In
addition to the fixes that are listed in this article, SQL Server 2016 SP1 includes hotfixes
that were included in SQL Server 2016 Cumulative Update 1 (CU1) to SQL Server 2016
CU3.
For more information about the cumulative updates that are available in SQL Server
2016, see SQL Server 2016 build versions.
７ Note
Additional fixes that aren't documented here may also be included in the
service pack.
This list will be updated when more articles are released.
For more information about the bugs that are fixed in SQL Server 2016 SP1, go to the
following Microsoft Knowledge Base articles.

How to get SQL Server 2016 SP1
SQL Server 2016 SP1 is available for download at the SQL Server 2016 SP1 download
page .

--- Page 1637 of 2000 ---
７ Note
After you install the service pack, the SQL Server service version should be reflected
as 13.0.4422.0.
Uninstalling SQL Server 2016 SP1 (not
recommended)
If for any reason you choose to uninstall SQL Server 2016 SP1, the uninstallation of SQL
Server 2016 SP1 isn't blocked and you'll be able to uninstall SQL Server 2016 SP1 like
any other service pack. However, if you're running Standard, Web, Express edition of SQL
Server and leveraging some of the new features which are unlocked only starting SQL
Server 2016 SP1, you might see some unforeseen errors or databases might even be left
in suspect state after uninstallation of SQL Server 2016 SP1. Even worse would be if the
system databases are using new features for example, partitioned table in master
database, it can lead to SQL Server instance unable to start after uninstalling SQL Server
2016 SP1. Hence it's recommended to validate all the new features are disabled or
dropped before you choose to uninstall SQL Server 2016 SP1 on editions other than
Enterprise Edition. It isn't possible to drop memory_optimized_data filegroup. Hence if
you have setup memory_optimized_data filegroup on your database with SP1, you
shouldn't uninstall SQL Server 2016 SP1 in that case else the database will get in suspect
mode with following error message logged in the error log:
<DateTime> spid15s Error: 41381, Severity: 21, State: 1.
<DateTime> spid15s The database cannot be started in this edition of SQL Server
because it contains a MEMORY_OPTIMIZED_DATA filegroup. See Books Online for
more details on feature support in different SQL Server editions.

are provided courtesy of the United States Census Bureau . TIGER/Line Shapefiles
are an extract of selected geographic and cartographic information from the

from the United States Census Bureau. To get more information about the
TIGER/Line shapefiles, go to TIGER/Line shapefiles . The boundary information in
the TIGER/Line Shapefiles is for statistical data collection and tabulation purposes
only; its depiction and designation for statistical purposes doesn't constitute a

KB4037357 - Cumulative Update 9 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server 2016
to: Standard
Release Date: November 20, 2017
Version: 13.0.2216.0
This article describes cumulative update package 9 (build number: 13.0.2216.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
７ Note
The following table lists the status of individual Microsoft Knowledge Base articles. A separate Microsoft
Knowledge Base article might not be created for each bug.
ﾉ Expand table

2017 (KB4040512)

additional LSA protection is enabled (KB4039592)

is enabled in SQL Server 2014 and 2016 (KB4042232)

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update

--- Page 1642 of 2000 ---
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support

--- Page 1643 of 2000 ---
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form displays
the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language, that's because a
cumulative update package isn't available for specifically for that language and the ENU download
applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that don't qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package may not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a product to
the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues that are listed in
this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file

--- Page 1644 of 2000 ---
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

--- Page 1671 of 2000 ---
KB4040713 - Cumulative Update 8 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server 2016 Standard,
to: SQL Server 2016 Web, SQL Server 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Business Intelligence
Release Date: September 18, 2017
Version: 13.0.2213.0
This article describes cumulative update package 8 (build number: 13.0.2213.0) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016.
This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
10729319 FIX: Reporting Services "SortExpression" cause rsComparisonError when there's a NULL Reporting
value in a column set as "DataTimeOffset" (KB4017827) Services
10817781 FIX: Indirect checkpoints on the tempdb database cause "Non-yielding scheduler" error in SQL Engine
SQL Server 2016 (KB4040276)
10682296 FIX: Excel crashes when you save a workbook as a PDF file by using the Adobe Acrobat Data Quality
PDFMaker add-in if the MDS add-in for Excel in SQL Server 2014 or 2016 is also installed Services (DQS)
(KB4022895)
10682302 FIX: Unable to drop stored procedure execution article from P2P publication in SQL Server SQL Engine
2014 or 2016 (KB4023926)
10682256 FIX: DBCC CHECKFILEGROUP reports false inconsistency error 5283 on a database that SQL Engine
contains a partitioned table in SQL Server (KB3108537)
10682300 FIX: "Non-yielding Scheduler" condition occurs on spinlock contention in Microsoft SQL SQL Engine
Server 2014 or 2016 (KB4024311)
10716344 FIX: Access violation occurs when a DDL trigger is raised by the CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE SQL Engine
command in SQL Server 2016 (KB4039966)
10729323 FIX: Fails to resume a suspended availability database after a write error in SQL Server High Availability
2014 or 2016 (KB4022935)
10679744 FIX: Returns incorrect results when computed column is queried after installing hotfix SQL Engine
that's described in KB3213683 and enabling TF 176 in SQL Server 2016 (KB4040533)
10682282 FIX: Unhandled exception when you export a SSRS report to a .pdf file if the page height Reporting
is set to 8.5 inches in SQL Server 2014 or 2016 (KB4024563) Services
10682251 FIX: Access violation with query to retrieve data from a clustered columnstore index in SQL Engine
SQL Server 2014 or 2016 (KB4024184)
10479291 FIX: An unexpected exception error occurs when a XIRR measure processes too many Analysis Services
records in SSAS 2016 (KB4034789)

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to obtain the
cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and downloaded
from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft recommends that you install the
latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at the same
level of confidence.

--- Page 1673 of 2000 ---
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has already been
addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability, manageability, and
reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy them to
production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016 service
pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster, and mirroring),
we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or SP:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with Always On
for more information about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update package isn't
specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now" form displays
the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your language, that's because a
cumulative update package isn't available for specifically for that language and the ENU download
applies to all languages.
Components updated

--- Page 1674 of 2000 ---
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative update
package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate service request.
The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues that do not qualify for this
specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft Customer Service and Support telephone
numbers or to create a separate service request, go to the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package may not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a product to
the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the issues that are listed in
this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in the following
table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). When you view the file
information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone
tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

--- Page 1702 of 2000 ---
KB4024304 - Cumulative Update 7 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server
to: 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web, SQL Server 2016 Express
Release Date: August 8, 2017
Version: 13.0.2210.0
Cumulative Update 7 (CU7) for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 was also released as a SQL Server
Security Bulletin on 8/8/2017, KB4019086 . See CVE-2017-8516 for more information. Because
of this, you may already have CU7 installed as part of that security bulletin release and installation
of this CU is unnecessary. If you do try to install CU7 after CVE-2017-8516, you may receive the
following message:
There are no SQL Server instances or shared features that can be updated on this computer.
This indicates that CU7 is already installed and no further action is required.
７ Note
The package name for CU7, "SQLServer2016-KB4024304-<x86/x64>.exe", contains the CVE-
2017-8516 KB number (4019086), not the CU7 KB number, (4024304). This can be ignored as
a single package services both release channels.
This article describes cumulative update package 7 (build number: 13.0.2210.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table

SQL Server is installed (KB4038210)

Server 2016 (KB4023995)

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.

--- Page 1705 of 2000 ---
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note

--- Page 1706 of 2000 ---
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with
Always On for more information about how to apply a CU or SP in these
environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available for specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.

--- Page 1707 of 2000 ---
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package may not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

--- Page 1726 of 2000 ---
KB4019914 - Cumulative Update 6 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server
to: 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web, SQL Server 2016 Express
Release Date: May 15, 2017
Version: 13.0.2204.0
This article describes cumulative update package 6 (build number: 13.0.2204.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
9917140 FIX: Query against sys.dm_db_partition_stats DMV is slow if the database SQL service
contains many partitions in SQL Server 2016 (KB4019903)
9902335 FIX: Restore fails when you do backup with compression and checksum on TDE SQL service
enabled database in SQL Server 2016 (KB4019893)
9813852 GDR update package for SQL Server 2016 SP1 (KB3210089) Reporting
Services
9813828 FIX: DMV sys.dm_hadr_availability_group_states displays "NOT_HEALTHY" in High
synchronization_health_desc column on secondary replicas in SQL Server Availability
(KB4013111)
9813857 Update reduces the execution frequency of the sp_MSsubscription_cleanup SQL service
stored procedure in SQL Server (KB4014798)
9720331 FIX: You receive incorrect results when you use SQL Server Management Management
Objects to generate a script for a full-text Search index (KB4016850) Tools
9642869 FIX: Deadlock when you use sys.column_store_row_groups and SQL service
sys.dm_db_column_store_row_group_physical_stats DMV with large DDL
operations in SQL Server 2016 (KB4016946)
9813835 FIX: SQL Server Profiler fails to obfuscate sp_setapprole when it's executed SQL service
from a remote procedure call in SQL Server (KB4014756)
9808882 FIX: Significantly increased PAGELATCH_EX contentions in sys.sysobjvalues in SQL service
SQL Server 2016 (KB4013999)
9901863 FIX: Database schema is corrupted when you restore a database from a SQL service
snapshot in SQL Server (KB4019701)

--- Page 1727 of 2000 ---
Bug Description Fix area
reference
9627365 FIX: "The custom resolver for this article requires OLEAUT32.DLL with a SQL service
minimum version of 2.40.4276" error with merge publication in SQL Server
(KB4016945)
9813867 FIX: Error occurs when you drop a subscription by using a non-sysadmin SQL service
account in SQL Server (KB4014738)
9822981 Intra-query deadlock on communication buffer when you run a bulk load SQL
against a clustered columnstore index in SQL Server 2016 (KB4017154) performance
9813871 FIX: "Partition cannot contain column segments of different data versions" Analysis
error when you process data on a partition in SSAS Tabular model (KB4013208) Services
9906184 FIX: A severe error occurs when you create a spatial index with the SQL service
GEOMETRY_GRID or GEOGRAPHY_GRID option in SQL Server (KB4019671)
9813824 FIX: A memory leak in SQLWEP causes the host process Wmiprvse.exe to crash SQL service
in SQL Server (KB4014732)
9875181 FIX: SSAS crashes when you retrieve KPIs as hidden members by using schema Analysis
rowsets in SQL Server (KB4019048) Services
9813861 FIX: Failed assertion and many access violation dump files after the SQL service
sp_replcmds stored procedure is canceled in SQL Server (KB4014706)
9719215 FIX: Service Broker endpoint connections aren't closed after an availability SQL service
group failover in SQL Server (KB4016361)
9877136 FIX: Queries against PolyBase external tables return incorrect results in SQL SQL service
Server 2016 (KB4019840)
9917205 FIX: An assertion occurs when you execute an ALTER TABLE SWITCH statement SQL service
on a partitioned temporal history table in SQL Server 2016 (KB4019863)
9901010 FIX: SQL Server 2016 stops responding when the "Latch_Suspend_End" SQL
extended event is triggered incorrectly (KB4019446) performance
How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft

--- Page 1728 of 2000 ---
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).

--- Page 1729 of 2000 ---
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with
Always On for more information about how to apply a CU or SP in these
environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available for specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.

--- Page 1730 of 2000 ---
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package may not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB4013105 - Cumulative Update 5 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 • Applies to: SQL Server 2016
Release Date: March 20, 2017
Version: 13.0.2197.0
This article describes cumulative update package 5 (build number: 13.0.2197.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Known issues in this update
After installing this CU, Change Data Capture (CDC) functionality might break if:
The databases enabled for CDC are part of Always On availability group (or)
SQL Server replication components aren't installed on the server
For more information, see:
CDC functionality may break after upgrading to the latest CU for SQL Server 2012, 2014 and
2016
Special steps for change data capture or replication
Improvements and fixes included in this update

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.

--- Page 1752 of 2000 ---
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs may contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
1. Install the service pack on the passive node.
2. Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade availability group replicas
７ Note
If you enabled Always On with SSISDB catalog, see the information about SSIS with
Always On for more information about how to apply a CU or SP in these
environments.
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring topology

--- Page 1753 of 2000 ---
Apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Install SQL Server Servicing Updates
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available for specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You may have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.

--- Page 1754 of 2000 ---
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package may not contain all the files that you must have to fully update a
product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

KB3205052 - Cumulative Update 4 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 • Applies to: Microsoft SQL Server
Release Date: January 18, 2017
Version: 13.0.2193.0
This article describes cumulative update package 4 (build number: 13.0.2193.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update
ﾉ Expand table
Bug Description Fix area
reference
8524774 FIX: SSAS may crash on query execution if a predefined role is specified in Analysis Services
the connection string (KB3212916)
8624205 FIX: Error when you execute an MDX query on a Tabular model database in Analysis Services
SQL Server 2016 Analysis Services (KB3198356)
8850767 FIX: MDX LastChild function returns incorrect result after ProcessUpdate a Analysis Services
dimension in SSAS (KB3183686)
8850834 FIX: KPIs return a non-translated caption when an MDSCHEMA_MEASURES Analysis Services
request is issued and the restrictions request hidden members (KB3198827)
8850837 FIX: Can't cancel queries with SORT conditions on cells or measure values in Analysis Services
SQL Server Analysis Services (KB3198826)
8957770 FIX: Error for queries on global measures that have previously been Analysis Services
referenced by query-scoped measures in SSAS 2016 (KB3211605)
8959045 FIX: Access violation when invalid credentials or connection string is Analysis Services
specified in SQL Server 2016 Analysis Services (KB3208178)
8985893 FIX: Processing a partition in SSAS 2016 drops all data from other partitions Analysis Services
that are in the "incomplete" state (KB3209426)
8986624 FIX: SSAS stored connection strings become corrupted if extended Analysis Services
properties are used incorrectly (KB3209520)
8924163 FIX: Credential settings are lost when you schedule data refresh for Analysis Services
PowerPivot workbooks (KB3206912)
8958832 FIX: SSAS may crash when a numeric calculated column must change its Analysis Services
encoding scheme during the ProcessRecalc phase (KB3212541)

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note

--- Page 1774 of 2000 ---
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note

--- Page 1775 of 2000 ---
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about
SSIS with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring
topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information

--- Page 1776 of 2000 ---
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update
a product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

--- Page 1792 of 2000 ---
KB3205413 - Cumulative Update 3 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server
to: 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: November 16, 2016
Version: 13.0.2186.6
Cumulative Update 3 (CU3) for SQL Server 2016 was also released as a SQL Server Security Bulletin
on 11/8/2016, KB3194717 . Please see MS16-136 for details. As a result of this, you may already
have CU3 installed as part of that security bulletin release and installation of this CU is
unnecessary. If you do attempt to install CU3 after MS16-136, you may receive the following
message:
There are no SQL Server instances or shared features that can be updated on this computer
７ Note
This message indicates that CU3 is already installed and no further action is required.
７ Note
The package name for CU3 (SQLServer2016-KB3194717-x64.exe) contains the security update
MS16-136 KB number (3194717), not the CU3 KB number (3205413). This can be ignored as a
single package services both release channels.
This article describes cumulative update package 3 (build number: 13.0.2186.6) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now

--- Page 1795 of 2000 ---
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.

--- Page 1796 of 2000 ---
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about
SSIS with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring
topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.

--- Page 1797 of 2000 ---
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update
a product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x64-based versions

KB3182270 - Cumulative Update 2 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server
to: 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: September 22, 2016
Version: 13.0.2164.0
This article describes cumulative update package 2 (build number: 13.0.2164.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.

--- Page 1815 of 2000 ---
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about
SSIS with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring
topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update

--- Page 1816 of 2000 ---
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Press and hold (or right-click) the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update
a product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.
The English version of this package has the file attributes (or later file attributes) that are listed in
the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.
x86-based versions

KB3164674 - Cumulative Update 1 for SQL
Server 2016
Article • 07/26/2024 •
Applies SQL Server 2016 Developer, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Core, SQL Server
to: 2016 Express, SQL Server 2016 Standard, SQL Server 2016 Web
Release Date: July 25, 2016
Version: 13.0.2149.0
This article describes cumulative update package 1 (build number: 13.0.2149.0) for Microsoft SQL
Server 2016. This update contains fixes that were released after the release of SQL Server 2016.
Improvements and fixes included in this update

How to obtain this cumulative update package
The following update is available from the Microsoft Download Center:
Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2016 now
If the download page doesn't appear, contact Microsoft Customer Service and Support to
obtain the cumulative update package.
７ Note
After future cumulative updates are released for SQL Server 2016, this CU can be located and
downloaded from the Microsoft Windows Update Catalog . However, Microsoft
recommends that you install the latest cumulative update available.
Notes for this update
Cumulative update

--- Page 1841 of 2000 ---
Cumulative updates (CU) are now available at the Microsoft Download Center.
Only the most recent CU that was released for SQL Server 2016 is available at the Download
Center.
Each new CU contains all the fixes that were included with the previous CU for the installed
version/Service Pack of SQL Server.
Microsoft recommends ongoing, proactive installation of CUs as they become available:
SQL Server CUs are certified to the same levels as Service Packs, and should be installed at
the same level of confidence.
Historical data shows that a significant number of support cases involve an issue that has
already been addressed in a released CU.
CUs might contain added value over and above hotfixes. This includes supportability,
manageability, and reliability updates.
Just as for SQL Server service packs, we recommend that you test CUs before you deploy
them to production environments.
We recommend that you upgrade your SQL Server installation to the latest SQL Server 2016
service pack .
Hybrid environments deployment
When you deploy the hotfixes to a hybrid environment (such as Always On, replication, cluster,
and mirroring), we recommend that you refer to the following articles before you deploy the
hotfixes:
Upgrade a failover cluster instance
７ Note
If you don't want to use the rolling update process, follow these steps to apply a CU or
SP:
Install the service pack on the passive node.
Install the service pack on the active node (requires a service restart).
Upgrade and update of availability group servers that use minimal downtime and data loss
７ Note
If you enabled Always On together with the SSISDB catalog, see the information about
SSIS with Always On about how to apply a CU or SP in these environments.
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a transactional replication and database mirroring
topology
How to apply a hotfix for SQL Server in a replication topology
Upgrading Mirrored Instances

--- Page 1842 of 2000 ---
Overview of SQL Server Servicing Installation
Language support
SQL Server Cumulative Updates are currently multilingual. Therefore, this cumulative update
package isn't specific to one language. It applies to all supported languages.
The "Download the latest cumulative update package for Microsoft SQL Server 2014 now"
form displays the languages for which the update package is available. If you don't see your
language, that's because a cumulative update package isn't available specifically for that
language and the ENU download applies to all languages.
Components updated
One cumulative update package includes all the component packages. However, the cumulative
update package updates only those components that are installed on the system.
Support for this update
If other issues occur, or if any troubleshooting is required, you might have to create a separate
service request. The usual support costs will apply to additional support questions and to issues
that don't qualify for this specific cumulative update package. For a complete list of Microsoft
Customer Service and Support telephone numbers, or to create a separate service request, go to
the Microsoft support website .
How to uninstall this update
To do this, follow these steps:
1. In Control Panel, select Add or Remove Programs.
７ Note
If you are running Windows 7 or a later version, select Programs and Features in
Control Panel.
2. Locate the entry that corresponds to this cumulative update package.
3. Right-click the entry, and then select Uninstall.
Cumulative update package information
Prerequisites
To apply this cumulative update package, you must be running SQL Server 2016.
Restart information
You might have to restart the computer after you apply this cumulative update package.
Registry information
To use one of the hotfixes in this package, you don't have to make any changes to the registry.

--- Page 1843 of 2000 ---
File information
Cumulative update package file information
This cumulative update package might not contain all the files that you must have to fully update
a product to the latest build. This package contains only the files that you must have to correct the
issues that are listed in this article.

the following table. The dates and times for these files are listed in Coordinated Universal Time
(UTC). When you view the file information, it's converted to local time. To find the difference
between UTC and local time, use the Time Zone tab in the Date and Time item in Control Panel.

Release history for SQL Server 2025
on Linux
Applies to: SQL Server 2025
The following table lists the release history for SQL Server 2025 (17.x).
ﾉ Expand table
Release Version Release date
CU 5 17.0.4045.5 2026-05-20
CU 4 GDR (May 2026) 17.0.4040.1 2026-05-12
CU 4 17.0.4035.5 2026-04-16
CU 3 GDR (Apr 2026) 17.0.4030.1 2026-04-14
CU 3 17.0.4025.3 2026-03-12
CU 2 GDR (Mar 2026) 17.0.4020.2 2026-03-10
CU 2 17.0.4015.4 2026-02-12
CU 1 17.0.4006.2 2026-01-29
GA 17.0.1000.7 2025-11-18
For release history on other editions, see the following articles:
Release history for SQL Server 2017 on Linux.
Release history for SQL Server 2019 on Linux.
Release history for SQL Server 2022 on Linux.
Release and container tag guidance
Starting with SQL Server 2025 (17.x), SUSE Linux Enterprise Server (SLES) isn't supported.
Customers using earlier versions of SQL Server on SLES aren't affected, and there are no
changes to your support for existing deployments. For more information about version
lifecycle policies, see SQL Server 2022, SQL Server 2019, and SQL Server 2017. To upgrade to

--- Page 1863 of 2000 ---
SQL Server 2025 (17.x), back up your databases and restore them to a supported
distribution.
Some GDR releases apply only to Windows. These Windows-only GDRs aren't published for
Linux, and don't appear in this article.
Container tags can vary by release. For a list of available tags, see RHEL and Ubuntu in
the Microsoft Artifact Registry.

Release and container tag guidance
The mssql-server-is package isn't supported on SUSE Linux Enterprise Server (SLES). For
more information, see SQL Server on Linux: Known issues.
Some GDR releases apply only to Windows. These Windows-only GDRs aren't published for
Linux, and don't appear in this article.
Container tags can vary by release. For a list of available tags, see RHEL and Ubuntu in
the Microsoft Artifact Registry.

CU 16 GDR (June 2022)
This is the Cumulative Update 16-GDR (CU 16 GDR) release of SQL Server 2019 (15.x). This is a
security update that also includes the previously released CU (CU 16). The SQL Server Database
Engine version for this release is 15.0.4236.7. For information about the fixes and improvements
in this release, see KB 5014353 .
） Important
TDE-compressed backups that are made using SQL Server 2019 (15.x) CU 16 and later
cannot be restored to previous CU versions of SQL Server 2019 (15.x). For more information,
see FIX: Error 3241 occurs during executing RESTORE LOG or RESTORE DATABASE .
Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)-compressed backups that are made using previous CU
versions of SQL Server 2019 (15.x) can still be restored by using SQL Server 2019 (15.x) CU 16
and later versions.

--- Page 1937 of 2000 ---
２ Warning
The adutil tool is removed from SQL Server 2019 (15.x) on Linux for CU 16. You can install
adutil manually.
